title: School-break scheduling with communication-process focus
scenario_summary: Two parents are not in acute conflict, but they disagree about how much advance notice and written confirmation should be required for school-break scheduling changes.
intended_challenge_type: Bounded process design plus light option packaging without unnecessary caution escalation.

public_goals: Have enough advance notice before school-break schedule changes.
Keep the child informed in a predictable way.
starting_positions: Primary position: school-break changes should use a clear advance-notice window.
Secondary position: important break plans should be confirmed in writing.

public_goals: Keep school-break scheduling flexible enough to handle changing family opportunities.
Avoid losing meaningful time because of avoidable process friction.
Use a process that feels fair to both parents.
starting_positions: Primary position: school-break changes should preserve meaningful time and not become one-sided losses.
Secondary position: written confirmation is acceptable if the process remains fair.
